reasoning: The Lean statement captures exactly the informal claim. It asserts the existence of a prime p dividing |G| = 351 together with a Sylow p-subgroup P that is normal. In Mathlib, `Sylow p G` is defined as a maximal p-subgroup, matching the standard mathematical definition. The hypothesis `hG : card G = 351` sets the order. The conjunction `p.Prime ∧ (p ∣ card G) ∧ P.Normal` correctly encodes "for some prime p dividing its order, there is a normal Sylow p-subgroup". The explicit `p ∣ card G` constraint prevents trivial solutions (for primes not dividing |G|, the trivial subgroup would be a Sylow p-subgroup that is trivially normal, which wouldn't match the spirit of the informal claim). The quantifier structure (∃p, ∃P, with conditions) matches the informal "for some prime p ... has a normal Sylow p-subgroup".
